MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Taylor Rottajakob appeared via zoom for Jeffrey Who (petitioner).

Mr. Rottajakob stated that he will be filing a declaration supporting the tenth account and the current trustee, First Republic Trust Company be replaced with the Peninsula Bay Trust as trustee. Eleventh account and report of trustees is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #10, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-28-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Taylor Rottjakob, via Zoom, for Jeffrey Wohl, Anne Marie Noonan, and First Republic Bank, co-Trustees.

Counsel confirms with the court the nature of the investments proposed in the petition with counsel advising of the intent to add diversity to the trust's investments. Counsel offers advocacy on behalf of the plan and reports of working closely with the trustees and investment advisor. The court reminds counsel to identify the class of investment especially if the expense ratios differ among the investments. The court confirms with counsel that she saw the Examiner's notes questioning the hourly attorney and paralegal rates with counsel advising that the firm's goal is to make the fee requests as efficient as possible.

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Evan Abrams for (co-trustees) Jeffrey Wohl, Anne Noonan & First Republic Trust Company.

The Court stated that it is concerned regarding the payment of attorney's fees without court approval for work on the trusts, and payment for the revocable trust by the special needs trust (SNT). Mr. Abrams stated that the SNT was set up for Sam and that the revocable trust was set up for Sam's benefit upon the death of the parents. The Court asked if this was not normal estate planning to be paid for by the trustors. Mr. Abrams stated that most estate plans do not need a complicated SNT, that he was not the estate planning attorney, that there are benefits to having the trust and that it was only set up to benefit the son. The Court stated that they are reaping the benefits and that it is concerned that they are using the SNT funds for their estate planning. Mr. Abrams stated that the same firm that did the conservatorship did the estate plan, that his firm obtains approval for fees they incur and that there seemed to be confusion regarding these other one time fees. The Court stated that it is not sure how there was confusion. Mr. Abrams stated that everything they do is for Sam's benefit. The Court stated that it is not sure it has timesheets for the attorney's fees and that if it is being asked to conclude that the fees were reasonable it will need timesheets. Mr. Evans stated that they will submit time sheets and that the fees seem reasonable. The Court continued the accounting petition to January 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 stating that it will need the timesheets and will need to consider the payment of the parents trust.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)
